mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in a single month
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above 0.3pp, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline model with a t-statistic below 1.5, or forward 60-day returns are negative in over 60% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production trigger immediate risk-off positioning as supply chain bottlenecks threaten corporate output, causing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of contraction episodes, or the mechanism shows zero predictive accuracy during supply disruptions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising core inflation combined with a stable or rising federal funds rate forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases > 0.3% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS does not decline
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in over 65% of high-inflation/stable-rate periods, or the interaction term yields a Rank IC below 0.02 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising wage income, which sustains corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The payroll growth signal produces a negative Rank IC over any 24-month rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (p > 0.05) during employment expansions.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real gross domestic product confirms economic contraction that immediately triggers portfolio de-risking, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 45% of GDP contraction quarters, or the signal fails to achieve statistical significance in event-study analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid expansion in industrial production during a low-unemployment regime signals overheating supply conditions that prompt central bank hawkishness, driving negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in over 60% of overheating episodes, or the signal yields a Rank IC below 0.01 during high-growth supply regimes.
